subject: FW: To Jeffrey re: NY mag reporter
body:
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 

________________________________

From: Tramo, Mark Jude [mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Friday, December 21, 2007 12:29 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To Jeffrey re: NY mag reporter



Hi Jeffrey - Hope you had a great Chanukah and are enjoying the holiday
season...ddealing w/ snow on ice hear in Mass...

Got a voicemail at my MGH office from a Phil Lice, who identified
himself as a reporter doing a profile on you for NY Magazine...would you
like me to call him back?

Looking forward to seeing you soon in Manhattan - am spending some time
w/ mom in the Bx before the start of the new year.

Best,
Mark
cellphone 781-718-6390
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu

c/o
LG


-----Original Message-----
From:   Lesley Groff [mailto:lesley@nysgllc.com]
Sent:   Wed 12/12/2007 3:58 PM
To:     Tramo, Mark Jude
Cc:    
Subject:        RE: Visit etc

I'm just glad Cady still loves Santa!



From: Tramo, Mark Jude [mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu]
Sent: Wednesday, December 12, 2007 3:51 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: RE: Visit etc



Thanks, Lesley - cellphone number below -I'll keep an ear (or two) out
for a call...I hope I have the opportunity to meet you in person this
next time around...

Yes, good ole Santa! - Cady (now 11) and I were just talking about Santa
when we saw one at the mall this weekend - she said she figured the
Easter Bunny wasn't real when she was about 6 and then, to her immediate
chagrin, she realized Santa was a myth...still loves him though!
(probably classical conditioning related, again, to the AG Store is
probably at play subliminally!)

Holiday Cheers!
Mark
781-718-6390

-----Original Message-----
From:   Lesley Groff [mailto:lesley@nysgllc.com]
Sent:   Wed 12/12/2007 11:20 AM
To:     Tramo, Mark Jude
Cc:   
Subject:        RE: Visit etc

What a nice trip you have planned with your family!  I do hope y'all
have a tremendous time!  I will let Jeffrey know you will be at the
Harvard club from (we'll say) Dec. 22-Jan 2/3.  Hopefully he will be
around and can catch up with you...and I'll mention New Year's night
too..but my guess is you are correct on where he will be!



(Nice to know the American Girl Store is good for something besides just
breaking the bank!  (I hear it's quite $$$$!)  I'm sure  Cady can spend
hours in there!....and yes, Christmas is going to just keep getting
better and better...already he really thinks Santa can see him...not a
lot of pouting going on around our house!)



J Les



From: Tramo, Mark Jude [mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu]
Sent: Wednesday, December 12, 2007 10:32 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: RE: Visit etc



Thanks so so much for everything, Lesley...

Here are the dates:
Will be coming down after my last patient day before the New Year - Fri
Dec 21st Cognitive Neurology clinic ends at 6P, then Candy, Cady and I
hop in the car, head to the Harvard Club, and stay in NYC until 1/2 or
1/3.

Would you like to choose a day and time in that time window today?

(So if history repeats itself I'll be able work out my American Girl
Store schedule w/ Cady well in advance!  I must admit she's learned a
fair amount of American History via their characters from c. 1775, 1865,
1929, 1944.)

And please extend an open invitation to Jeffrey-plus-guest to join Candy
and me for a drink-and-a-dance in Harvard Hall on New Year's Eve anytime
from 9P on...(though I can't imagine him choosing Manhattan over a
private island in St Thomas in January!)

Looking forward to your reply, and hope you're enjoying the Season all
the more thanks to your new set of toddler eyes!

Holiday Cheers,
Mark
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu
781-718-6390


-----Original Message-----
From:   Lesley Groff [mailto:lesley@nysgllc.com]
Sent:   Tue 12/11/2007 11:33 AM
To:     Tramo, Mark Jude
Cc:  
Subject:        RE: To LG

Hello again....regarding dates for the 2008 tutorial-Jeffrey said
starting Jan.



Regarding meeting up prior to xmas or prior to New Years-I think he
would really like to...he said 'possibly', give us a shout out as the
date approaches.



From: Tramo, Mark Jude [mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu]
Sent: Friday, December 07, 2007 9:07 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To LG



Hi, Lesley!

Any dates set yet so I can start planning trips/materials for my 2008
tutorial w/ JE in Florida?

I checked via Google to see if Landon Thomas' article is out yet - I
hadn't realized he was the writer of the "International Moneyman of
Mystery" piece in New York mag - must have come out around the time
Austin Powers was big, right?  I DID find, however, an article that had
a hugely complimentary bit about one Lesley Groff:
http://www.nytimes.com/2005/02/05/business/05support.html

Here's the info I promised to send re: future checks:

1.  "Mark Jude Tramo MD PhD", Taxpayer ID# 094-40-3637
Address: 87 Lafayette St, Marblehead, MA 01945
(re: 2008ff tutorial JE said he would take care of in January 2008 when
we spoke on Thanksgiving weekend)

2.  "The Institute for Music & Brain Science", Mass. Tax Exempt
#300-031-939
Address: c/o Dr Tramo, 87 Lafayette St, Marblehead, MA 01945
(re: $50k 2007 donation to Institute pledged during our 11/3 phone
conversation peri-Cold Spring Harbor/pre-Cambridge mtg)

Please let me know if you would advise that I make a phone appt w/ JE
next week to discuss the above and/or the large package of materials I
sent the week of Thanksgiving.

Lastly, I expect to be in Manhattan just before and after New Year's and
would love to see Jeffrey - will he be around?

Holiday Cheers!
Mark
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu

Mark Jude Tramo, MD, PhD
Executive Director, The Institute for Music & Brain Science
Dept of Neurology, Harvard Medical School & Massachusetts General
Hospital
Steering Committee, Harvard University Mind/Brain/Behavior Interfaculty
Initiative
Best Doctors in America(r)/ America's Top Physicians(r)/Best of
Boston(r)
Songwriter Member, ASCAP

Email  MTramo@HMS.Harvard.edu
URL  http://www.BrainMusic.org

END

Lesley Groff
